sketches.mechanism: Ownership transfer (move semantics) — after giving a value away, the original binding becomes inaccessible, enforced at compile time.
sketches.resonance: Giving yourself to someone means you can no longer use what you gave; the compiler enforces the irreversibility of devotion.

sketches.mechanism: The `Nothing` type, which is the bottom type — a function returning `Nothing` can never return, and a variable of type `Nothing` can never be instantiated.
sketches.resonance: A promise whose return type guarantees it will never be fulfilled — the type system itself makes arrival impossible.

sketches.mechanism: A type class instance for `Eq` that always returns False — two values can never be considered equal, despite having the same structure.
sketches.resonance: Two things that look identical but can never recognize each other; equality is defined as always failing.
sketches.draft:
data Us = Us

instance Eq Us where
  _ == _ = False

alone = Us == Us
